skill_areas.name: Batting Basics
skill_areas.description: Grip, stance, backlift, head position, weight distribution, and basic shot selection including front foot and back foot defense
pain_points: Incorrect grip (bottom hand too dominant) leading to lack of control
Head falling over or moving outside off stump, losing balance
Playing across the line to straight deliveries
Fear of short-pitched bowling causing flinching
Bat coming down at an angle instead of straight
common_mistakes: Gripping the bat too tightly causing stiff wrists
Standing too open or too side-on in stance
Backlift going towards second slip instead of over stumps
Weight stuck on back foot when playing front foot shots
Eyes not level at point of delivery
drills: Shadow batting for stance and backlift (15 min daily)
Throwdown practice for timing with half-volley lengths
Front foot drive against half-volley lengths
Defense drill - block 50 balls with focus on soft hands
Grip check drill with bat drop test

milestones: Can hold correct grip consistently for 30 minutes
Can defend balls on good length on both front and back foot
Can play straight drive with proper follow-through
Can leave balls outside off stump confidently

skill_areas.name: Front Foot Shots
skill_areas.description: Straight drive, cover drive, on drive, off drive, and front foot defense. Foundation shots for batting on good pitches.
pain_points: Not getting to the pitch of the ball
Head not over front knee causing mistimed shots
Playing away from body in cover drive
Closing bat face too early on on-drive
No follow-through resulting in lack of power
common_mistakes: Lunging at the ball instead of stepping smoothly
Front foot going towards leg stump (falling over)
Not presenting full face of bat for straight drive
Trying to hit too hard instead of timing the ball
Bottom hand dominant causing cross-bat shots
drills: Cone drill - place cone at driving length, step to it
Throwdowns on off stump for cover drive practice
Shadow drive with bat - 50 repetitions daily
Drive off bowling machine at medium pace
Partner drill - hit drives into target zones

milestones: Can play straight drive along the ground consistently
Can drive through covers with proper foot movement
Can differentiate between driving and defending length
Can play on-drive without closing the face

skill_areas.name: Back Foot Shots
skill_areas.description: Cut shot, pull shot, hook shot, back foot punch, and back foot defense. Essential for handling short-pitched bowling.
pain_points: Getting hit by short balls due to slow reaction
Top-edging pull shots leading to catches
Playing cut shot too close to body
Not rocking back far enough on back foot
Cannot differentiate between balls to pull vs leave
common_mistakes: Closing eyes when ball is short
Not getting on top of bounce for cut shot
Attempting pull shot to deliveries too close to body
Front foot not clearing to give room for cut shot
Head falling towards off side on pull shot
drills: Short ball machine drill - practice ducking and pulling
Cut shot off throwdowns from close range
Pull shot with tennis ball for confidence building
Back foot punch drill against good length balls
Reaction drill - coach calls shot type before delivery

milestones: Can pull short balls consistently along the ground
Can cut balls outside off with placement
Can defend on back foot with soft hands
Can safely duck/sway under bouncers

skill_areas.name: Power Hitting (T20 Skills)
skill_areas.description: Switch hits, ramp shots, lofted drives, slog sweeps, reverse sweeps, and 360-degree batting for T20 and ODI formats
pain_points: Mistiming aerial shots leading to catches
Cannot clear the boundary consistently
Poor shot selection under pressure in death overs
Lack of range - can only score in limited areas
Cannot hit against the spin effectively
common_mistakes: Trying to hit every ball for six
Swinging too hard and losing shape
Not using the crease to create room
Ignoring singles while looking for boundaries
Pre-determining shots before seeing the ball
drills: Slog sweep against spin bowling machine
Upper cut practice with short ball machine
Ramp/scoop shot drill against pace (start with tennis ball)
Switch hit practice against throwdowns
Target hitting - place cones at different boundary sections
Free hitting after facing 6 dot balls (pressure drill)

milestones: Can clear 60m boundary with lofted drive
Can play switch hit off both spin and pace
Can execute scoop/ramp with controlled direction
Can score at 150+ strike rate for 5 overs in nets

skill_areas.name: T20 Match Batting
skill_areas.description: Match-specific T20 batting skills including powerplay aggression, middle overs rotation, death overs finishing, and batting under DLS scenarios
pain_points: Cannot accelerate at the right time
Getting bogged down in middle overs
Failing to finish innings with a flourish
Poor understanding of DLS target adjustments
Cannot read match situations for shot selection
common_mistakes: Playing too many dot balls trying to settle in
Not adapting to pitch conditions mid-innings
Ignoring field placements when choosing shots
Not using batting depth to take calculated risks
Playing the same way regardless of match phase
drills: Scenario-based net practice (need 15 off 6 balls)
Middle overs simulation - score at 8 RPO without boundaries
Death overs finishing drill - last 3 overs practice
Musical chairs batting - rotate strike every ball drill
Match simulation with bowling machine changing lengths

milestones: Can bat through a T20 innings in simulated match
Can accelerate from 100 SR to 180+ SR on demand
Can finish an innings with 15+ runs in last over
Can identify match phases and adjust approach

skill_areas.name: Playing Spin Bowling
skill_areas.description: Techniques for facing off-spin, leg-spin, and variations. Reading spin from the hand, using feet, sweep options, and defensive technique against spin.
pain_points: Cannot read spin direction from bowler's hand
Stuck in crease - no footwork to get to pitch
Getting stumped when charging down the track
Playing for spin that does not exist (bat-pad catches)
Cannot sweep consistently against quality spinners
common_mistakes: Planting front foot before seeing length
Playing with hard hands leading to catches at short leg
Not watching the ball onto the bat
Using only pad to defend instead of bat-pad combination
Not sweeping when spinner is bowling tight on leg stump
drills: Ball identification drill - call spin direction before bounce
Charging drill - practice dancing down and driving spin
Sweep shot variations against side-arm throwdowns
Defending on back foot against spin with soft hands
Facing spinners with different actions (over/round the wicket)

milestones: Can read off-spin vs leg-spin from bowler's hand
Can use feet to get to the pitch of the ball
Can sweep and reverse sweep with control
Can defend on turning pitches without edging

skill_areas.name: Playing Pace Bowling
skill_areas.description: Handling 130+ km/h deliveries, trigger movements, playing late, leaving outside off, and survival techniques against quality fast bowling.
pain_points: Late on deliveries - ball hits pad or misses bat
Poking at balls outside off stump
Cannot handle steep bounce from tall bowlers
Flinching or backing away from fast bowling
No score rotation against disciplined fast bowling
common_mistakes: Trigger movement too early or too large
Bat coming down too late for front foot shots
Chasing wide deliveries outside off stump
Standing too tall in stance against pace
Not watching the ball from bowler's hand to bat
drills: Bowling machine at 130+ km/h for eye adjustment
Side-arm practice to simulate pace and bounce
Leave drill - practice leaving balls outside off channel
Short ball drill - duck, sway, and pull progressions
Fast hands drill with rapid throwdowns from 15m

milestones: Can face 130+ km/h comfortably in nets
Can leave balls on judgment outside off stump
Can pull and cut short balls safely
Can drive full deliveries from fast bowlers

skill_areas.name: Building an Innings
skill_areas.description: The art of pacing an innings - getting set, rotating strike, converting starts to big scores, accelerating at the right time, and match awareness.
pain_points: Getting out between 20-40 consistently (conversion problem)
Cannot rotate strike - too many dot balls
Playing a rash shot after getting set
Not reading match situation correctly
Fatigue affecting concentration after 50+ balls
common_mistakes: Trying to accelerate too early in innings
Not having a scoring plan against each bowler
Ignoring easy singles while waiting for boundaries
Losing concentration after reaching a milestone (50/100)
Not adjusting to changing pitch conditions
drills: Long batting drill - bat for 60+ minutes in nets
Strike rotation drill - take single off every ball in over
Phase batting - practice getting through first 20 balls
Acceleration drill - increase scoring rate every 10 balls
Match simulation with scoreboard pressure

milestones: Can bat 30+ minutes in nets without getting out
Can rotate strike against all bowling types
Conversion rate of 30+ scores to 50+ improves
Can identify when to attack and when to consolidate

skill_areas.name: Powerplay Batting (Overs 1-6)
skill_areas.description: Strategies and shot selection for batting in the powerplay with fielding restrictions. Opening batting in T20 and ODI formats.
pain_points: Getting out in powerplay trying to be aggressive
Scoring too slowly in powerplay wasting field restrictions
Cannot handle new ball swing in first 5 overs
Poor shot selection against new ball bowlers
Not using gaps in field during powerplay
common_mistakes: Trying to hit boundaries from ball one without judging conditions
Not using the pace of the ball for boundaries
Ignoring fielding restrictions and scoring options
Playing the same way on different pitches
Not targeting weaker bowlers in powerplay
drills: Powerplay simulation - 6 overs with new ball
Gap hitting drill - exploit areas where fielders cannot be placed
Against-swing batting practice in first 3 overs
Lofted shots over infield practice
Match awareness drill - scoring at 8+ RPO for 6 overs

milestones: Can score at 8+ RPO in powerplay practice
Can handle new ball movement while scoring
Can identify and exploit powerplay field gaps
Dismissal rate in powerplay reduces over time

skill_areas.name: Fast Bowling
skill_areas.description: Run-up, delivery stride, seam position, swing bowling (outswing, inswing, reverse swing), pace generation, and bowling action fundamentals
pain_points: No-balls from overstepping crease
Losing pace due to poor body alignment
Cannot generate outswing or inswing consistently
Injury risk from incorrect action (stress fractures)
Cannot bowl a consistent line and length
common_mistakes: Mixed bowling action (front-on shoulders with side-on hips)
Run-up too long causing deceleration before crease
Head falling away at delivery point
Front arm pulling away too early
No follow-through after release
drills: Standing start delivery drill for action correction
Seam position practice with target at different positions
Bound and delivery stride drill from 5-step run-up
Slower ball variations practice (back of hand, knuckle, cutter)
Yorker practice with target on crease line
Run-up marking and consistency drill

milestones: Can bowl 6 overs without a no-ball
Can hit good length consistently (6 out of 6)
Can bowl outswing with upright seam
Can generate 120+ km/h pace

skill_areas.name: Swing Bowling (Outswing, Inswing, Reverse)
skill_areas.description: Mastering conventional swing (outswing and inswing) and reverse swing bowling. Seam position, wrist angle, release point, and conditions for swing.
pain_points: Ball does not swing despite correct seam position
Cannot control swing direction consistently
Losing pace while trying to swing the ball
No reverse swing with old ball
Swing disappearing after first few overs
common_mistakes: Seam not upright at release - wobbly seam
Wrist behind the ball instead of angled for swing
Bowling too short for swing to take effect
Not shining the ball correctly for conventional swing
Releasing too early or too late for desired movement
drills: Seam position drill with slow delivery focus
Outswing corridor bowling (4th-5th stump line)
Inswing with angled wrist - target leg stump
Old ball reverse swing practice after 40+ overs
Swing test drill - bowl 6 balls checking movement direction

milestones: Can swing ball both ways on demand
Can maintain swing for 4+ overs
Can bowl reverse swing with old ball
Can combine swing with consistent line and length

skill_areas.name: Seam Bowling
skill_areas.description: Upright seam, cross seam, wobble seam, and seam movement off the pitch. Medium-fast bowling technique and variations.
pain_points: Seam landing position inconsistent
Cannot generate seam movement on flat pitches
Cross seam resulting in half-volleys
Ball not hitting seam on pitching
Cannot vary pace while maintaining seam position
common_mistakes: Releasing with scrambled seam instead of upright
Not using the crease for angle variation
Bowling same pace every ball
Wrist behind ball (no seam presentation)
Not adjusting seam position for different pitch types
drills: Upright seam release practice at reduced pace
Cross seam bounce variation drill
Wobble seam grip and release practice
Seam hitting drill - pitch ball on target and check seam impact
Crease variation drill - bowl from wide and close to stumps

milestones: Can land ball on seam 4 out of 6 deliveries
Can bowl cross seam for extra bounce on demand
Can vary pace by 10+ km/h while keeping seam upright
Can use wobble seam as variation

skill_areas.name: Death Bowling (Last 5 Overs)
skill_areas.description: Yorker execution, slower ball variations (back of hand, knuckle, leg cutter, off cutter), wide yorkers, and strategies for bowling at the death in limited-overs cricket.
pain_points: Cannot execute yorker under pressure
Slower balls getting picked and hit for six
Full tosses instead of yorkers (slot balls)
No variation - batsman reads bowling pattern
Leaking 15+ runs per over at the death
common_mistakes: Bowling same slower ball variation repeatedly
Aiming at stumps instead of toes for yorker
Reducing pace too much on slower balls
Not having a plan B when yorkers miss target
Bowling to batsman's strengths instead of weaknesses
drills: Yorker target practice - hit base of stumps 50 times
Slower ball variations rotation (6 balls, 6 different types)
Wide yorker practice outside off stump
Pressure death over simulation - score under 10 runs
Bouncer-yorker combination drill for set batsmen

milestones: Can execute yorker 4 out of 6 attempts
Has 3+ slower ball variations
Can bowl death overs at under 10 RPO average
Can execute wide yorker on demand

skill_areas.name: Spin Bowling
skill_areas.description: Off-spin, leg-spin, left-arm orthodox, googly, doosra, carrom ball, top spinner, flipper, flight and loop control
pain_points: Cannot turn the ball consistently
No control over length and line while spinning
Getting hit for boundaries when tossing up for flight
Cannot bowl the googly/doosra without telegraphing it
Ball not dipping on approach to batsman
common_mistakes: Bowling too fast - not giving ball enough revolutions
Releasing with flat trajectory (no loop/flight)
Bowling too short allowing easy back foot play
Same arm speed for stock ball and variations
Wrist position changing visibly between deliveries
drills: Spinning the ball from hand without run-up (100 reps daily)
Target zone bowling on a length (6 balls in pizza box)
Flight and dip practice with variable pace at same length
Variation bowling - stock, googly, top spinner rotation
Bowling against batsman in nets with field set

milestones: Can turn the ball consistently on any surface
Can bowl 4 overs for under 30 runs in practice match
Can bowl googly/variation without action change
Can control flight and get the ball to dip

skill_areas.name: Fielding and Ground Work
skill_areas.description: Ground fielding, sliding stops, throwing accuracy, relay throwing, attacking the ball, and fielding positions understanding
pain_points: Poor throwing accuracy to keeper/bowler's end
Hesitation in approaching the ball (fear of misfield)
Cannot throw flat and hard from boundary
Slow ground fielding pickup technique
Not backing up throws from other fielders
common_mistakes: Not bending low enough for ground fielding
Throwing off wrong foot (losing momentum)
Standing flat-footed instead of being on toes
Not attacking the ball on approach
Picking up with wrong hand for throwing direction
drills: Rapid ground fielding and throw drill (20 reps)
Sliding stop practice on both sides
Throwing accuracy - hit single stump from 25m, 30m, 40m
Relay throw drill with partner
Chase and retrieve drill for boundary fielding

milestones: Can hit stumps directly 3 out of 5 throws from 30m
Can execute sliding stop and throw in one motion
Can field 20 balls without a misfield
Understands all standard fielding positions

skill_areas.name: Catching (Slip, Outfield, High)
skill_areas.description: Slip catching technique, outfield catching, high catches under lights, catching in the deep, and close-in catching at short leg and silly point.
pain_points: Dropping catches under pressure in match situations
Misjudging high catches in the outfield
Hard hands in slip cordon causing drops
Cannot catch with sun/floodlights in eyes
Poor reaction time at close-in positions
common_mistakes: Snatching at the ball instead of receiving it
Not getting into correct ready position
Running backwards for high catches instead of turning
Watching fielders instead of ball in slip cordon
Fingers pointing up for catches below waist (should point down)
drills: Slip catching cradle practice (50 catches per session)
High catch drill with catching machine
Outfield catch - run and judge trajectory drill
Close-in catching reaction drill with tennis ball
Catching under floodlights practice for day-night games

milestones: Can take 8 out of 10 slip catches in practice
Can judge and catch high balls consistently
Can take catches while running in outfield
Can react and catch at short leg from 3m

skill_areas.name: Wicketkeeping
skill_areas.description: Stance, footwork, glovework to pace and spin, standing up and standing back, stumping technique, and diving catches
pain_points: Late reactions to outside edges off fast bowlers
Poor footwork when standing up to spinners
Fumbling on leg-side takes and leg-side stumpings
Standing up too close or too far from stumps
Cannot take diving catches consistently
common_mistakes: Rising too early from crouch before delivery
Moving head before ball arrives (pre-empting direction)
Gloves not in correct position for receiving
Not giving with the ball on catch (hard hands)
Poor positioning for stumping - reaching instead of moving feet
drills: Tennis ball reaction drill (20 catches from 10m)
Standing up to spin with tennis ball for confidence
Lateral dive and catch drill (both sides, 10 each)
Stumping practice with spin bowler in nets
One-handed catch drill for diving takes
Footwork drill - move to 6 different positions

milestones: Can keep to pace bowlers without byes for 10 overs
Can stand up to medium-pace bowlers comfortably
Can execute stumping off spin within 0.5 seconds
Can take diving catches on both sides

skill_areas.name: Running Between Wickets
skill_areas.description: Calling conventions, turning technique, grounding the bat, judging quick singles, running twos, and building partnerships through rotation
pain_points: Poor calling leading to run-outs and mix-ups
Not grounding bat on turns (losing time)
Slow first 2 steps out of crease
Misjudging fielder arm strength and position
Not backing up at non-striker's end
common_mistakes: Both batsmen calling at same time (confusion)
Running with bat in air instead of dragging/grounding
Turning blind (not looking at ball while turning)
Not running the first run hard (lazy running)
Ball-watching instead of being ready to run
drills: Sprint and turn drill with bat (10 x 22 yards)
Calling and communication drill with partner
Quick single judgment drill with fielder
Ground bat drill - practice sliding bat at crease
Backing up drill at non-striker's end

milestones: Can complete a single in under 3 seconds
Zero run-out mix-ups in 5 consecutive practice matches
Can convert 1s to 2s with efficient turning
Calling is clear and early every time

skill_areas.name: Captaincy and Match Tactics
skill_areas.description: Field placement, bowling changes, batting order decisions, DRS usage, reading match situations, and tactical awareness across formats
pain_points: Cannot set effective fields for different bowlers
Poor DRS decisions wasting reviews
Wrong bowling changes at crucial moments
Not adapting tactics to pitch and conditions
Cannot motivate team under pressure
common_mistakes: Setting defensive fields too early in innings
Not using part-time bowlers when conditions suit
Reviewing decisions based on emotion, not judgment
Keeping same field for different batsmen
Not communicating plans to bowlers clearly
drills: Field setting exercise on tactics board
Watch match footage and pause to decide bowling change
DRS decision simulation with ball tracking data
Scenario exercise - set field for death overs with 3 options
Communication drill - explain bowling plan to each bowler

milestones: Can set attacking and defensive fields for each bowler
DRS success rate above 50% in practice scenarios
Can read match situations and adjust tactics
Team responds positively to tactical communication

skill_areas.name: Middle Overs Strategy (Overs 11-40)
skill_areas.description: Batting and bowling strategies for middle overs in ODIs and T20s. Strike rotation, partnership building, spin bowling plans, and pressure building.
pain_points: Scoring rate dropping too low in middle overs
Losing wickets in clusters during consolidation phase
Spinners getting hit when building pressure
Cannot rotate strike against tight bowling
Partnership breakdowns in middle overs
common_mistakes: Trying to accelerate before a platform is built
Not identifying which bowler to target
Batsmen scoring at same pace (no roles defined)
Spinners bowling too defensively in middle overs
Not using the reverse sweep or sweep to rotate
drills: Middle overs batting simulation (score 5-7 RPO for 10 overs)
Partnership role play - one anchor, one aggressor
Spin bowling in middle overs with field set
Strike rotation drill against spin
Targeting specific bowler in middle overs practice

milestones: Can maintain 6+ RPO in middle overs practice
Can build partnerships with defined roles
Spinner can bowl 5 overs for under 35 in middle period
Can identify and target weak bowling in middle overs

skill_areas.name: Cricket Fitness
skill_areas.description: Physical conditioning for cricket including the BCCI Yo-Yo test preparation, agility, speed, endurance, strength training, and injury prevention.
pain_points: Failing BCCI Yo-Yo fitness test (16.1 score required)
Cramping during long batting or fielding sessions
Bowling speed dropping after 4-5 overs due to fatigue
Recurring injuries (hamstring, back, shoulder)
Cannot maintain concentration for full day in Test cricket
common_mistakes: Only running for fitness (ignoring strength and agility)
Not warming up properly before matches and nets
Skipping cool-down and stretching after sessions
Not hydrating enough in Indian heat conditions
Training too hard before match days
drills: Yo-Yo test preparation interval training (3x per week)
Agility ladder drills for fielding quickness
Core strength workout for batting and bowling stability
Shoulder strengthening for fast bowlers (rotator cuff)
Sprint and recovery intervals (20m x 10 sets)
Flexibility and mobility routine (daily 15 min)

milestones: Can achieve Yo-Yo test score of 16.1+
Can field for 50 overs without fatigue
Bowling pace maintained for 8+ overs
No recurring injuries over 3 month period

skill_areas.name: Mental Preparation and Pressure Handling
skill_areas.description: Sports psychology for cricket - handling pressure, pre-match routines, visualization, dealing with failure, concentration techniques, and mental toughness.
pain_points: Choking under pressure in crucial match situations
Cannot recover from a bad shot or dropped catch
Negative self-talk after getting out cheaply
Pre-match anxiety affecting performance
Loss of concentration during long innings or fielding sessions
common_mistakes: Thinking about previous delivery while facing next one
Setting unrealistic expectations before a match
Not having a consistent pre-delivery routine
Dwelling on failures instead of learning from them
Comparing performance with teammates or opponents
drills: Visualization exercise - batting for 10 minutes with eyes closed
Breathing technique for between deliveries (4-7-8 method)
Positive self-talk affirmation before batting
Concentration drill - face 6 balls fully focused, reset
Post-match reflection journal (what went well, what to improve)
Pressure simulation - crowd noise during net practice

milestones: Has consistent pre-delivery routine in place
Can reset after a bad ball within 10 seconds
Maintains focus for 30+ minutes continuously
Performs consistently in practice matches under pressure

skill_areas.name: Net Practice Techniques
skill_areas.description: How to structure net sessions for maximum improvement. Goal-setting for practice, scenario-based drills, and self-assessment techniques.
pain_points: Wasting net sessions without specific goals
Just slogging in nets instead of practicing technique
Not practicing match scenarios in nets
Not recording and reviewing net sessions
No plan for what to work on each session
common_mistakes: Batting 30 minutes without a plan
Only facing friendly bowling in nets
Not practicing weaknesses (only playing strengths)
Bowlers not bowling with intent in nets
No feedback mechanism - practicing same mistakes
drills: Session planning - write 3 goals before entering nets
Scenario batting - 'last 5 overs need 50 runs'
Video recording of 10 balls and self-review
Weakness-focused session - 70% time on weak areas
Match simulation net with umpire and scoring

milestones: Every net session has written goals
Can review own technique from video recordings
Practices match scenarios at least once per week
Shows measurable improvement in weak areas over 4 weeks

aggressive_batsman.description: Fearless stroke-makers who dominate bowling attacks. Focus on power hitting, T20 batting, and back foot shots for short ball handling.

anchor_batsman.description: Backbone of any batting lineup. Build innings, rotate strike, convert starts to big scores, and bat through the innings.

pace_bowler.description: Speed merchants and swing artists. Develop pace, swing, seam movement, and death bowling variations.

spin_specialist.description: Masters of turn, flight, and deception. Develop spin variations, control, and tactical bowling in middle overs.

all_rounder.description: Complete cricketers who contribute with bat, ball, and in the field. Balance skill development across disciplines.

wicketkeeper_batsman.description: Dual-role specialists who keep wickets and contribute with the bat. Modern keepers are expected to be dynamic batsmen.

beginner.description: New cricketers should build sound batting technique, reliable catching, smart running between wickets, and learn how to practice effectively.

intermediate.description: Developing cricketers should specialize in a bowling type, refine shot-making, and learn to handle quality bowling.

advanced.description: Competitive players should develop T20 batting skills, advanced bowling variations, tactical awareness, and mental toughness.
